title: salvia lavandulifolia subsp. vellerea
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. vellerea, also known as red salvia or vellerea salvia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, characterized by its red to purple flowers and its native habitat in the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. vellerea include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety due to its sedative properties. Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. vellerea can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
